title: EndpointContext
description: Per-request context handed to a handler. Mirrors agtp.handlers.EndpointContext. Every field has been validated by agtpd before crossing the gateway: input is schema-checked, agent_id is authenticated, authority_scope is claim-validated.

input.description: The validated request body. Required fields are guaranteed present; optional fields may be absent. Types match the endpoint's input schema.

agent_id.description: The invoking agent's identity, from Agent-ID (or legacy Target-Agent). Empty string for server-level probes.

principal_id.description: Identifier of the human or entity the agent acts on behalf of. Empty when not declared.

agent_scopes.description: Scopes the calling agent has declared. Surfaced for finer-grained checks; the dispatcher already enforced required_scopes.

authority_scope.description: §10 Authority-Scope header: scopes claimed for this specific request. Pre-validated against the agent's declared scope set.

session_id.description: §10 Session-ID — opaque operational grouping. Passed through without server-side interpretation.

task_id.description: §10 Task-ID — task tracing across requests. Echoed back by agtpd in the response automatically.

request_id.description: Correlation id matching the Request-Id response header. Used for log correlation.

method.description: The AGTP verb (uppercase ASCII). Validated against the method catalog before this envelope was constructed.

path.description: The URI path the request targeted. Validated against AGTP path grammar.

headers.description: Request headers, keys lowercased. For handlers that need access to e.g. Idempotency-Key or Trace-Parent.
